teamObjective.explanation: My hand is all low non-trump spades (3–6) with no trumps, so I cannot expect to dominate the table. The best team-oriented goal is to lead the weakest card, making it cheap for my partner to win the trick and take control.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner has 3 unknown cards. Given that I hold four low non-trump cards, the remaining deck is richer in higher cards and trumps, so partner has a non-negligible chance of holding at least one card that can beat a 3 of spades. Partner is likely to try to take the lead if able.

opponentBelief.summary: The two opponents jointly hold 6 unknown cards. With no public cards played, it is probable that at least one opponent holds a trump (rank 7 or jokers) or a higher non-trump single that can beat my lead. Opponents will likely try to win the trick to seize the lead.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the 3 of spades is the cheapest possible lead. It signals a weak hand, avoids committing my highest card, and gives my partner the best chance to overtake with a modest card while preserving the 6 for a later trick.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Leading the 6 uses my strongest card on a weak hand. It is still very vulnerable to any trump or higher non-trump, and if it loses I am left with only 3–5, reducing my future options.
The 4 is only marginally stronger than the 3 and does not materially change the chance of winning the trick, while sacrificing the ability to keep the 4 as a higher exit card later.
The 5 has the same problem as the 4: it is unlikely to beat opponents or partner responses, and leading it unnecessarily weakens my remaining hand.

risks: Opponent 1 may play a higher non-trump or any trump and win the lead before my partner can act.
My partner may be unable to beat the card that wins the trick, leaving the opponents in control.
Leading low reveals that I have little high-card strength.
riskAssessment.mitigation: By leading the absolute lowest card, I minimize the value I put at risk and keep the 6 as a potential later winner. The low lead also maximizes the set of cards my partner can use to take control.
